labels: Dobhoff tube terminates within the stomach.
report:
=== VISUAL_FINDINGS ===
A single portable view of the lower chest and upper abdomen shows an enteric tube with its tip in the upper stomach. 

A relatively distended loop of small bowel is visible in the mid-abdomen. 
Excreted contrast is seen within the renal pelvises bilaterally.

Lateral aspect of the left lower hemi-thorax is excluded from the examination, but the other pleural surfaces appear normal. 
The lungs are clear. 
Borderline cardiomegaly remains unchanged. 
Incidental note of large lymph node calcifications in the mediastinum and right hilus.

=== CLINICAL_EVIDENCE ===
Retrieved literature states that an enteric tube placed in the upper stomach can be confirmed by visualization of the tube's tip at the gastro-esophageal junction or beyond.

=== DEDUCTIVE_reasoning ===
Step 1: The presence of the enteric tube with its tip in the upper stomach correlates with the clinical evidence that it was placed in this location.

Step 2: The visibility of the distended loop of small bowel supports the placement of the enteric tube, as it would likely cause obstruction if not properly positioned.
